docs.title: Katalytikertagung – Abstract Metadata
docs.text:
This site documents the metadata extraction pipeline and search widget for the DECHEMA Katalytikertagung (German Catalysis Conference) abstract collection.

The dataset currently contains 217 approved abstracts from the 2025 conference.

docs.title: Search Abstracts
docs.text:
Search by title, author, or affiliation. Click Read abstract → to open the original PDF inline.

More options

See Search Options for compact and accordion variants, along with copy-paste <iframe> embed codes for all three.

docs.title: About this project
docs.text:
The abstracts are processed through a three-step pipeline:

  1. Extract — batch PDF extraction via a local LLM (Ollama) → staging.json
  2. Review — tkinter GUI for metadata validation and affiliation normalisation → abstract_metadata.json
  3. Publish — this documentation site, auto-generated from the approved dataset

The pipeline is maintained by NFDI4Cat. See the How To section for usage instructions.

docs.title: Search Widget – Embed Options
docs.text: Three variants of the search widget are available, each self-contained HTML file served via GitHub Pages. Choose the one that fits your website context and embed it with the <iframe> code shown below.

docs.title: Option 1 — Compact
docs.text:
A minimal two-line result row: bold title + year on the first line, presenting author and truncated co-author list on the second line.

Best for: tight spaces, sidebars, or pages where brevity is important.

Preview:

Embed code: 

<iframe
  src="https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/Kat_Tagung_Metadata_extraction/widgets/search_option_1.html"
  width="100%"
  height="440"
  frameborder="0">
</iframe>

docs.title: Option 2 — Accordion
docs.text:
Each result shows title + presenting author collapsed. Click any row to expand the full author list with affiliations.

Best for: pages where you want to keep the initial view compact but still allow users to dig into author details without leaving the page.

Preview:

Embed code: 

<iframe
  src="https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/Kat_Tagung_Metadata_extraction/widgets/search_option_2.html"
  width="100%"
  height="440"
  frameborder="0">
</iframe>

docs.title: Option 3 — Full Details + PDF Reader
docs.text:
Each result shows title, presenting author, full author list with institutions, and a Read abstract → button that opens the original PDF inline in a reader page.

Best for: the primary conference page where users should have full access to all information and the ability to read the original abstract.

Preview:

Embed code: 

<iframe
  src="https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/Kat_Tagung_Metadata_extraction/widgets/search_option_3.html"
  width="100%"
  height="520"
  frameborder="0">
</iframe>

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text: Option 1 Option 2 Option 3 Title + year ✓ ✓ ✓ Presenting author truncated ✓ ✓ Full author list truncated on expand ✓ Affiliations truncated on expand ✓ PDF reader link — — ✓ Recommended height 440 px 440 px 520 px

docs.title: Updating the widget
docs.text:
After approving new abstracts in the review GUI, regenerate all widget files:

py search_widget/build.py

Then commit and push — GitHub Pages updates within seconds.

docs.title: How To
docs.text:
Documentation for the Katalytikertagung abstract metadata pipeline.

Guide Description Use the Search Widget How to search abstracts on the website Add New Abstracts How to process a new batch of PDFs Script Reference CLI options for all pipeline scripts

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
New PDFs  →  1_extract.py  →  staging.json  →  2_review_gui.py  →  abstract_metadata.json
                                                                           ↓
                                                               build.py  →  index.html (live)

docs.title: Step 1 — Place PDFs in the abstracts folder
docs.text: Copy the new PDF files into data/abstracts/. Filenames should be the anonymised identifiers (e.g. 52001_abstract.pdf) — they are never displayed to end users.

docs.title: Step 2 — Run the extraction script
docs.text:
py scripts/pipeline/1_extract.py \
    --input   data/abstracts/ \
    --model   llama3.2 \
    --issued  2026-10-12 \
    --skip-existing
  • --issued sets the dct:issued date for this batch (conference date).
  • --skip-existing skips PDFs that are already in staging.json or abstract_metadata.json.
  • Results are appended to data/staging.json with _status: "pending".

Ollama must be running

Start Ollama before running the script: ollama serve The default model is llama3.2; change with --model NAME.

docs.title: Step 3 — Review and approve in the GUI
docs.text:
py scripts/pipeline/2_review_gui.py

For each staged entry:

  1. Compare the extracted metadata against the original PDF (image + text panel).
  2. Edit any incorrect fields directly.
  3. Resolve each affiliation decision:
    • Existing — map to a known canonical institution name.
    • New — register a new canonical name (the registry is updated on approval).
  4. Click Approve to move the entry to abstract_metadata.json.

Failed extractions

Entries where Ollama failed to extract valid JSON appear with a [!] icon. Open them in the GUI, fill in the fields manually, and approve.

docs.title: Step 4 — Rebuild the search widget
docs.text:
py search_widget/build.py

This regenerates all three widget variants and the reader page with the updated dataset.

docs.title: Step 5 — Commit and push
docs.text:
git add data/abstract_metadata.json data/affiliation_registry.json \
        docs/widgets/ search_widget/index.html
git commit -m "Add abstracts from [conference] [year]"
git push

GitHub Pages updates within a few seconds of the push.

docs.title: scripts/pipeline/1_extract.py — Batch PDF extraction
docs.text:
Processes PDFs with a local Ollama model and writes results to data/staging.json.

py scripts/pipeline/1_extract.py [OPTIONS]
Option Default Description --input PATH data/abstracts/ Folder of PDFs to process --model NAME llama3.2 Ollama model name --issued DATE today dct:issued date for this batch (YYYY-MM-DD) --skip-existing off Skip PDFs already present in staging or approved --dry-run off List matching PDFs without calling Ollama

Output per PDF:

  • [N/M] file_title OK — successfully extracted
  • [N/M] file_title SKIP — already exists (with --skip-existing)
  • [N/M] file_title FAIL — Ollama returned invalid JSON after 2 attempts

docs.title: scripts/pipeline/2_review_gui.py — Review & management GUI
docs.text:
py scripts/pipeline/2_review_gui.py

Staged mode — review staging.json entries:

Action Effect Approve Moves entry to abstract_metadata.json; updates affiliation_registry.json Discard Permanently removes entry from staging.json Skip Advances to next entry without changes

Approved mode — manage abstract_metadata.json entries:

Action Effect Save Writes edited fields back to abstract_metadata.json Delete Removes entry from abstract_metadata.json

Search filters: Title / Author / Affiliation (radio buttons in the left pane).

Granular deletion (within an entry):

  • Remove individual author rows with the Remove author button.
  • Remove individual affiliation strings with the x button per row.

docs.title: scripts/pipeline/shared.py — Shared utilities (migration CLI)
docs.text:
Not normally run directly. One-time migration from the legacy intermediate files:

py scripts/pipeline/shared.py --migrate

Converts:

  • data/intermediate/metadata_output_*_cleaned_affiliations3.json → data/abstract_metadata.json
  • data/intermediate/affiliation_groups3.json → data/affiliation_registry.json

docs.title: search_widget/build.py — Generate widget HTML files
docs.text:
py search_widget/build.py

Reads data/abstract_metadata.json and writes:

File Description docs/widgets/search_option_1.html Compact variant docs/widgets/search_option_2.html Accordion variant docs/widgets/search_option_3.html Full details + PDF reader link docs/widgets/reader.html Abstract reader page (metadata + PDF embed) search_widget/index.html Backward-compat copy of option 1

Run this script every time abstract_metadata.json is updated, then commit the generated files.

docs.title: Install MkDocs
docs.text: pip install mkdocs-material

docs.title: Local preview
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Opens a live-reload preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Deploy to GitHub Pages
docs.text:
mkdocs gh-deploy

Builds the site and pushes it to the gh-pages branch. The MkDocs hook (scripts/mkdocs_hooks.py) automatically copies PDFs from data/abstracts/ into the build so they are served at /abstracts/*.pdf.

docs.title: Other scripts
docs.text: Script Purpose scripts/normalize_ttl_affiliations.py One-time TTL URI normalisation (already applied) scripts/delete_abstracts.py Legacy deletion tool for the RDF/TTL file ontology/widoco_mergerand_modifier_00.py Widoco documentation pre-processor ontology/widoco_postmodifier_01.py Widoco documentation post-processor

docs.title: How to Use the Search Widget
Basic search
docs.text:
Type any word or phrase into the search box. Results update as you type.

The search runs across title, author names, and affiliations simultaneously (fuzzy matching — minor typos are tolerated).

docs.title: Filtering by field
docs.text:
Use the radio buttons below the search box to restrict results:

Filter Searches in All Title + author names + affiliations Title Abstract title only Author Author names only Affiliation Institution names only

docs.title: Reading an abstract
docs.text:
In the Full Details widget (Option 3), each result has a Read abstract → button. Clicking it opens the original PDF in an inline reader page showing:

  • Full title and year
  • Presenting author (highlighted)
  • All authors with their affiliations
  • The PDF embedded directly in the page

docs.title: Clearing the search
docs.text: Click the × button on the right side of the search box, or select all and delete.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Searching "Berlin" under Affiliation finds all abstracts with a Berlin-based author.
  • Searching a surname under Author is the fastest way to find a specific person's work.
  • The search is case-insensitive.
